name: Recruitis.io
description:
# API Documentation for the Recruitis.io System
The documentation is based on authorization using a token, which is generated either by the system itself or by the user within Recruitis.

Over time, additional functionalities will be added. If the functionality of any API call is disrupted,
a new branch (v2, etc.) will be created, or you will be informed of the change well in advance.

### API URL

To call the API, use the URL: [https://app.recruitis.io/api2/](https://app.recruitis.io/api2/).

### Changelog

All changes in the documentation are recorded in the [Changelog](#tag/Changelog).

### Important Notice:

When testing a POST request on the production server: the default token points to a test account accessible by other users.  
**Therefore, do not send your personal data using this token; instead, replace it with your own token.**

## Tokens
Authorization tokens are divided into two groups, each with two levels.

### Token Groups

#### Company Tokens

This type of token has the advantage that it is not tied to a specific account, so it will not stop working if the account is deleted or deactivated.
However, not all API calls can be used with this token. (Each API call will specify whether it supports a company token.)
Some calls require knowing the exact author (for reporting, for example), and unfortunately, those cannot be used with a company token.

#### User-Specific Tokens

Each user can create their own token in Recruitis. With this token, all API calls can be used, including those
that need to be linked to a specific account.

### Tokens by Permission Levels:

+ `full_access`: A token with full privileges, allowing modification of data in the company’s system. This token must **never** be publicly exposed.
+ `public_access`: A token with mostly read-only privileges. This token can be publicly exposed, for example when using JavaScript.

A domain whitelist can be applied to both types of tokens (we recommend enabling this setting).

Tokens can be created after logging into Recruitis: [*settings* > *API integration*](https://app.recruitis.io/zadavatel/nastaveni/obecne/view/api).

Authorization itself is performed by sending a header: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

_Example:_ `Authorization: Bearer 30bcaf299e100318906fef2f442505216fa28af1.11196.99382ec77e2a79ac2af2671730817ec6`.

## Meta Information

Each `response` contains a `meta` section that provides all supporting information.  
The attributes listed below **may not** always be present in the meta section and may depend on the circumstances of the call (`entries_*` values appear in the meta section only when a payload array is returned, etc.).

| META parameter      | Note                                                                                  |
|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `code`              | Result code. In case of error, it is a standardized error message.                     |
| `message`           | A message describing the call.                                                         |
| `duration`          | Elapsed processing time for the request.                                               |
| `cached`            | The result is cached and may not be up to date.                                        |
| `cached_from`       | The generation time of the returned result.                                            |
| `deprecated`        | The call is marked as deprecated and should no longer be used.                         |
| `additional_output` | See separate section.                                                                  |
| `entries_from`      | When returning an array, indicates the starting element (*used for pagination*).       |
| `entries_to`        | When returning an array, indicates the ending element (*used for pagination*).         |
| `entries_total`     | When returning an array, indicates the total number of elements (*used for pagination*).|
| `entries_sum`       | When returning an array, indicates the current number of elements (*used for pagination*).|

### Meta Information - `additional_output`

When uploading new data (answers, candidates), situations may occur where the entity is saved with certain exceptions  
(duplicate attachments, candidate merging, GDPR anonymization, invalid attachments). In such cases, the API informs you of everything that happened with the entity, see the example below. This state is also logged on our server and can be traced later.

```json
{
  "additional_output": [
    {
      "message": "Candidate already exists. I'll use his user ID.",
      "status": "WARNING",
      "code": "candidate.put.exists.join"
    },
    {
      "message": "File cv.txt is already uploaded.",
      "status": "WARNING",
      "code": "candidate.put.attachment.duplicated"
    },
    {
      "message": "File logo.jpg is already uploaded.",
      "status": "WARNING",
      "code": "candidate.put.attachment.duplicated"
    },
    {
      "message": "File souhlas.pdf is already uploaded.",
      "status": "WARNING",
      "code": "candidate.put.attachment.duplicated"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Description of the code Response Parameter

Each response has a `code` parameter. Here is the list of values and their meaning:

| CODE parameter                                   | Note
|:-------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| `api.ok`                                         | The request was successful. 
| `api.found`                                      | The request found a result and returned it.
| `api.created`                                    | The request created an entity.
| `api.deleted`                                    | The request deleted an entity.
| `api.modified`                                   | The request modified an entity.
| `api.unmodified`                                 | The request made no new changes, returned an existing entity.
| `api.response.null`                              | The request succeeded but returned no result. 
| `api.error.generic`                              | General error. Unspecified.
| `api.error.system.unavailable`                   | The API system is unavailable.
| `api.error.request.wrong_format`                 | Invalid JSON request format.
| `api.error.request.property.missing`             | A required value is missing in the request.
| `api.error.request.property.wrong_value`         | A variable in the request has an invalid value. 
| `api.error.request.property.wrong_text_length`   | Text length in the request exceeds the allowed value.
| `api.error.request.property.maximum_array_items` | The number of array items exceeds the allowed limit.
| `api.error.not_found`                            | The request targets a non-existent resource. 
| `api.error.unauthorized`                         | The request requires authentication.
| `api.error.authorization.low_privileges`         | The request requires `full_access` token.
| `api.error.authorization.blocked`                | Login temporarily blocked (e.g., too many failed login attempts). 
| `api.error.authorization.wrong_token_format`     | The Bearer token is in an invalid format.
| `api.error.authorization.token.doesnt_exists`    | The provided Bearer token does not exist. 
| `api.error.authorization.wrong_credentials`      | Incorrect login credentials. 
| `api.error.authorization.bad_domain`             | The API call comes from an unauthorized domain.
| `api.error.quota.exceeded`                       | Daily/minute call limit reached, see [Quotas](#quotas).
| `api.error.account.deleted`                      | The account you are trying to access has been deleted. 
| `api.error.account.banned`                       | The account you are trying to access has been permanently banned. 
| `api.error.upload.generic`                       | Error occurred while uploading an attachment. 
| `api.error.upload.size`                          | The attachment exceeds upload size limit.
| `api.error.upload.timeout`                       | Timeout when retrieving the file from external storage. File unavailable.
| `api.error.upload.bad_host`                      | You are trying to upload a file that does not exist. 
| `api.error.upload.no_data`                       | You are trying to upload a file with no data. 
| `api.error.user.already_answered`                | The candidate has already answered the job ad.
| `api.error.response.too_big`                     | The response is too large.
| `api.error.export.bad_request`                   | General error during job export to another system.
| `api.error.referral.already_created`             | The referral has already been created.
| `api.error.job.disallowed_acess_type`            | This type of job does not accept responses or cannot be modified.

## Additional Notes on API Calls

### Quotas

Quotas monitor each type of system call, tracking their frequency. When a limit is reached, the given call type is blocked.  
If the limit is reached, the response will return `403 - forbidden` with `meta.code` = `api.error.quota.exceeded`.

Quotas will be updated and optimized over time. 

Current list of quotas:

| Quota name                | Quota type | Max value | Note                                        |
|:--------------------------|------------|-----------|---------------------------------------------|
| `quota_candidates_delete` | daily      | 100       | Maximum number of candidates that can be deleted daily. |

### Parameters

If a parameter is marked as `array` in the documentation, the system expects the parameter in the URL either as a single value (`foo=bar`)  
or as an array (`foo[]=bar&foo[]=baz`).

#### Query Parameters

Query parameters are appended to the end of the request in the format:

`?parameter_name1=value1&parameter_name2=value2`

Example:

`?limit=25&page=2&job_id=30665&job_id=23982`

### Attachment Types

Attachment types are used when creating an answer or candidate.

| Value  |  Note                           |
|:-------|---------------------------------|
| `1`    | General attachment
| `2`    | CV attachment
| `3`    | Email correspondence (obsolete)
| `4`    | Photograph
| `5`    | GDPR consent
| `6`    | Profile picture

### Special Tag Types

If you insert one of the special tags below instead of a regular tag value, it has a deeper effect on candidate handling.

| Value        | Note                                                                 |
|:-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `tag.hot`    | This tag, when added to a candidate (via answer/candidate creation), marks the candidate as a "hot candidate" visible on the main page.  

### Parameter Value Formats

 **date** - A string in the format YYYY-MM-DD (2018-01-30)  

 **datetime** - A string in the format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS (2018-01-30 12:59:59)
